title: System for analyzing tissue
abstract: A system for analyzing tissue includes a platform and an optical sensing unit coupled to the platform. The optical sensing unit has a detector and a plurality of light sources surrounding and electrically isolated from the detector. The optical sensing units obtain optical data for tissue analysis.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A system for analyzing tissue, said system comprising: a platform; and an optical sensing unit coupled to said platform with said optical sensing unit having a detector and a plurality of light sources surrounding and electrically isolated from said detector, said optical sensing unit obtaining optical data for tissue analysis. 2. The system as set forth in claim 1 wherein said plurality of light sources includes at least one first light source and at least one second light source, and said detector being disposed between said first and second light sources. 3. The system as set forth in claim 1 or 2 wherein said platform is a microprobe and said system further comprises a tissue aspiration needle with said microprobe being carried by said tissue aspiration needle. 4. The system as set forth in claim 3 wherein said microprobe defines a longitudinal axis and said optical sensing unit is one of a plurality of optical sensing units coupled to said microprobe with said plurality of optical sensing units being linearly arranged along said longitudinal axis. 5. The system as set forth in claim 4 wherein said plurality of optical sensing units includes at least two optical sensing units. 6. The system as set forth in claim 4 wherein said plurality of optical sensing units includes from two to twenty optical sensing units. 7. The system as set forth in any one of claims 4-6 wherein said microprobe has first and second opposing surfaces and said plurality of optical sensing units 

 includes at least one optical sensing unit coupled to each of said first and second opposing surfaces of said microprobe. 8. The system as set forth in claim 1 or 2 wherein said platform is a flexible sheet having first and second opposed surfaces and said optical sensing unit is one of a plurality of optical sensing units coupled to at least one of said first and second opposed surfaces of said flexible sheet. 9. The system as set forth in claim 8 wherein said plurality of optical sensing units is arranged on at least one of said first and second opposed surfaces of said flexible sheet according to a predefined pattern. 10. The system as set forth in claim 8 wherein said plurality of optical sensing units is randomly arranged on at least one of said first and second opposed surfaces of said flexible sheet. 1 1. The system as set forth in any of the preceding claims wherein each of said first and second light sources is a Î¼ÎÎ¤ÏÏ emitting light within the visible spectrum. 12. The system as set forth in claim 1 1 wherein said first light source is a Î¼Î ^ ÎÎ emitting a red light and said second light source is a Î¼ÎÎ¤ÏÏ emitting a blue light. 13. The system as set forth in any of the preceding claims wherein said detector is chosen from a photodiode, a photodetector, and a phototransistor and said detector has a peak spectroscopic sensitivity of from about 850 to 900 nm. 14. The system as set forth in any of the preceding claims wherein said first and second light sources are separated by a distance from about 0.4 to 5 mm, 

 measured from the center of said first light source to the center of said second light source. 15. The system as set forth in any of the preceding claims wherein said optical data is reflectance data. 16. The system as set forth in claim 15 further comprising a computer including an application containing computer-readable instructions causing the computer to utilize said reflectance data to analyze the tissue. 17. The system as set forth in any one of claims 1-14 wherein said optical data is fluorescence data and said system further comprises a filter adjacent said detector for cutting a wavelength of light for measuring said fluorescence data. 18. A system for analyzing tissue, said system comprising: a tissue aspiration needle defining a cavity having an opening; and a microprobe disposed within said cavity and carried by said tissue aspiration needle with said microprobe defining a longitudinal axis and having a platform and a plurality of optical sensing units coupled to said platform and linearly arranged along said longitudinal axis, said optical sensing units being exposed through said opening of said cavity, with each of said sensing units having first and second light sources and a detector arranged between and electrically isolated from said first and second light sources; wherein said plurality of sensing units obtain optical data substantially simultaneously for analyzing the tissue. 19. The system as set forth in claim 18 wherein said first light source is a Î¼Î ^ ÎÎ emitting a red light and said second light source is a Î¼ÎÎ¤ÏÏ emitting a blue light. 20. The system as set forth in claim 18 or 19 wherein said microprobe further includes a printed circuit board coupled to said platform, and said plurality of optical sensing units coupled to said printed circuit board. 21. A method of analyzing tissue utilizing a system comprising a platform and an optical sensing unit coupled to said platform with the optical sensing unit having a detector and a plurality of light sources surrounding and electrically isolated from the detector, said method comprising the steps of: directing a light from the light sources toward the tissue; obtaining optical data while the light is being directed toward the tissue; utilizing the optical data to analyze the tissue. 22. The method as set forth in claim 21 wherein the plurality of light sources includes a first light source and a second light source, and wherein the step of directing the light is further defined as directing a red light from the first light source and a blue light from the second light source. 23. The method as set forth in claim 21 or 22 wherein the step of obtaining the optical data is further defined as obtaining optical reflectance data. 24. The method as set forth in claim 21 or 22 wherein the system further includes a filter adj acent the detector and the step of obtaining the optical data is further defined as obtaining fluorescence data.
